title: IP Addressing, DNS, DHCP, NAT, VLAN, and VPN · Evidence Workshop
summary: A guided support bench extends layered IP configuration and dependency trace while retaining authorization, safety, baseline, privacy, accessibility, rollback, verification, documentation, and user communication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of layered IP configuration and dependency trace while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret IPv4 and IPv6 address, prefix, host, subnet, gateway, loopback, link-local, private, public, dynamic, and static roles.
Trace discovery, lease, reservation, exclusion, scope, options, renewal, relay, conflict, fallback, and authoritative ownership.
Trace local names, hosts data, client cache, configured resolver, recursive and authoritative answers, record types, TTL, search suffixes, and application caching.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Addressing Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Duplicate addresses, stale names, a guest isolation leak, an unreachable subnet, and VPN split behavior are all blamed on DNS. The workshop variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Interpret IPv4 and IPv6 address, prefix, host, subnet, gateway, loopback, link-local, private, public, dynamic, and static roles.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Addressing Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for interpret ipv4 and ipv6 address, prefix, host, subnet, gateway, loopback, link-local, private, public, dynamic, and static roles. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Addressing Model, keep Addressing Model connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Duplicate addresses, stale names, a guest isolation leak, an unreachable subnet, and VPN split behavior are all blamed on DNS. The workshop variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Interpret IPv4 and IPv6 address, prefix, host, subnet, gateway, loopback, link-local, private, public, dynamic, and static roles.

content.value: CompTIA A+ Core 1 220-1201 Exam Objectives — Controls every current Core 1 mobile, networking, hardware, virtualization, cloud, and troubleshooting objective and weighting.

lines.label: assumption

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: required

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Vpn Evidence.
